title: The Invisible Man - Curriculum Unit Card 👁️

notes: Launch-ready Grade 8 card with reading, vocabulary, journals, writing, questions, informational text, and assessments.

book.title: The Invisible Man - Curriculum Unit Card 👁️
book.description: Grade Level: 8th Grade (adaptable to 7th-9th) Duration: 15 days (12 reading days + 3 assessments) Genre: Science Fiction / Psychological Thriller Theme: Power, Isolation, Consequences of Ambition Curriculum Days: 31-45 (Quarter 1, Weeks 7-9)

weeks.title: Week 7: The Invisible Man - Curriculum Unit Card 👁️ Part 1 (Days 31-35)
reading: Chapters 1-8: The Invisible Man - Curriculum Unit Card 👁️
vocabulary: Science ethics: define, use in context, and connect to the text
literary evidence: embed and explain a quotation
academic vocabulary: analyze author craft and theme
journal: What does this section reveal about Science ethics? Use one quotation or scene as evidence.
Which character choice, conflict, or image feels most important so far? Explain why.
writing: Write an analytical paragraph about setting, conflict, or character introduction.
Strengthen one response by adding a precise quotation and commentary.
questions: What conflict or question is driving this section of the book?
How does the author use setting, mood, structure, or point of view?
Which quotation best supports your interpretation of a character or theme?
How does this section connect to the larger quarter theme?
assessment: Weekly comprehension check, vocabulary application, and short analytical response.
Review day: discussion, revision, and evidence check.

weeks.title: Week 8: The Invisible Man - Curriculum Unit Card 👁️ Part 2 (Days 36-40)
reading: Chapters 9-16: The Invisible Man - Curriculum Unit Card 👁️
vocabulary: Isolation: define, use in context, and connect to the text
literary evidence: embed and explain a quotation
academic vocabulary: analyze author craft and theme
journal: What does this section reveal about Isolation? Use one quotation or scene as evidence.
Which character choice, conflict, or image feels most important so far? Explain why.
writing: Write a comparison paragraph connecting the fiction to the paired informational text.
Strengthen one response by adding a precise quotation and commentary.
questions: What conflict or question is driving this section of the book?
How does the author use setting, mood, structure, or point of view?
Which quotation best supports your interpretation of a character or theme?
How does this section connect to the larger quarter theme?
informationalText: Isolation and mental health in literature
assessment: Weekly comprehension check, vocabulary application, and short analytical response.
Review day: discussion, revision, and evidence check.

weeks.title: Week 9: The Invisible Man - Curriculum Unit Card 👁️ Part 3 (Days 41-45)
reading: Chapters 17-24: The Invisible Man - Curriculum Unit Card 👁️
vocabulary: Power: define, use in context, and connect to the text
literary evidence: embed and explain a quotation
academic vocabulary: analyze author craft and theme
journal: What does this section reveal about Power? Use one quotation or scene as evidence.
Which character choice, conflict, or image feels most important so far? Explain why.
writing: Write a short literary analysis paragraph about theme, author craft, or moral complexity.
Revise one paragraph into the end-of-card portfolio response.
questions: What conflict or question is driving this section of the book?
How does the author use setting, mood, structure, or point of view?
Which quotation best supports your interpretation of a character or theme?
How does this section connect to the larger quarter theme?
assessment: Weekly comprehension check, vocabulary application, and short analytical response.
Book-card assessment: passage analysis, vocabulary, and literary analysis paragraph.
